coordination_status.description:
Report this replica's writer-lease role and coordinator health.

Read-only and safe during coordinator outages. Credentials and vault content
are never included.

adopt_vault.description:
Adopt an existing vault safely without replacing originals.

Default mode scans only. Copy/compile modes write under the governed
Knowledge Base layer and preserve original path/hash provenance.

selected_paths.description: Explicit legacy files for copy/compile modes.

adoption_studio.description:
Run a governed, resumable Adoption Studio session over existing material.

Adoption Studio turns a messy legacy vault into governed Exomem knowledge
without ever rewriting, moving, or deleting an original file. It is a durable,
canonical-file-backed run with a preview-exact-actions contract: you see the
precise imports before anything is written, and `apply` commits exactly that
plan or refuses. One required `action` multiplexes the whole lifecycle; the
read-only default (`status`) is safe and `start` is explicitly guarded.

Lifecycle actions: `start` scans a subtree read-only and snapshots a candidate
inventory; `select` materializes a folder-rule selection server-side; `plan`
previews exact targets, titles, hashes, and frontmatter; `apply` copies the
validated subset into governed Sources with provenance in one atomic batch;
`cancel` closes a pre-apply run; `finish` proves recall and hands you a first
question. Agent actions ride the run afterwards: `work-item` returns bounded
read-only context, `propose` submits structured proposals, and
`apply-proposal` approves one through an existing governed leaf.

action.description: Required. One of start, status, select, plan, apply, cancel, finish, work-item, propose, or apply-proposal.

run_id.description: Stable adoption run id from `start`; required by every action except `start` and the run-listing form of `status`.

path.description: For `start`, the vault subtree to scan. Defaults to the vault root.

include_hidden.description: For `start`, include hidden files/directories in the scan.

initialize_kb.description: For `start`, bootstrap the Knowledge Base scaffold first when it does not exist yet (otherwise `start` refuses with KB_NOT_INITIALIZED).

include.description: For `select`, folder or file paths whose eligible files are selected (server materializes the concrete set).

exclude.description: For `select`, folder or file paths to remove from the selection.

overrides.description: For `select`, explicit per-file paths to force-select.

include_junk.description: For `select`, include junk (e.g. zero-byte) files that are otherwise demoted. Default false.

plan_id.description: For `apply`, the plan id echoed from `plan`/`status`; a mismatch or a changed selection is refused with PLAN_STALE.

retry_failed.description: For `apply`, re-plan and re-apply only the failed subset.

only_paths.description: For `apply`, restrict the (retry) apply to these originals.

why.description: Required approver rationale for `apply-proposal`; also records the reason on `cancel`.

write_manifest.description: For `finish`, write an optional run manifest under `Knowledge Base/_Adoption/`. Default true.

sources.description: For `work-item`, explicit source paths to include instead of the first `max_sources` applied imports.

max_sources.description: For `work-item`, the maximum sources returned. Default 5.

max_chars_per_source.description: For `work-item`, the per-source excerpt cap. Default 2000.

proposals.description: For `propose`, the list of structured proposal objects to submit.

expected_fingerprint.description: For `apply-proposal`, the reviewed fingerprint that must still match, or the write is refused.

expected_hash.description: For `apply-proposal`, the target page hash for relation and reconciliation-relate approvals.

ask_memory.description:
Recall durable knowledge from Exomem with product defaults.

This is the normal first read: search compiled knowledge, sources,
evidence, media sidecars, and curated vault files without making the
caller choose internal primitives. Set `deep=true` to return a packed
reasoning context instead of only hits. Heavy behavior stays explicit:
rerank is only forced when `rerank=true`, and graph enrichment is only
requested when `graph_enrich=true`.

query.description: Question or search phrase. Empty means recent/filtered recall.

file_types.description: Optional artifact kind filters such as pdf, image, csv, json.

categories.description: Semantic-unit category shortcuts, such as config or rule.

kinds.description: Semantic-unit kind shortcuts, such as decision or claim.

deep.description: Return a packed context for reasoning.

graph.description: Include graph-neighbour ranking in hybrid/vector search.

rerank.description: Force or suppress cross-encoder reranking; omit for mode-aware auto.

prefer_compiled.description: Prefer compiled notes over raw sources by default.

prefer_active.description: Prefer active conclusions over superseded ones.

prefer_used.description: Apply usage boost when explicitly requested.

graph_enrich.description: With deep mode, include typed graph neighborhood data.

bootstrap.description:
Return Exomem's versioned operating contract for generic MCP clients.

Call this once at the start of a session when the client does not have the
Exomem Claude Skill loaded. It teaches the agent how to use the tools: when
to search, when to save, how to interpret scoped misses, which `find` knobs
are cheap vs diagnostic, how compiled notes differ from raw sources/evidence,
and how Exomem differs from built-in AI memory. The payload is deterministic
instruction plus local compute policy and product-surface metadata; it does
not inspect or summarize vault content.

profile.description: "compact" (default), "full", or "diagnostics". Compact is enough for normal clients. Full adds examples. Diagnostics adds performance interpretation guidance.

workflow.description: Optional caller-selected workflow label. Returned as context only; it does not change server behavior.

browse_memory.description:
Browse vault structure without reading many files.

`mode="overview"` returns a bounded product adoption/structure report.
`mode="list"` returns entries for a folder. Both are read-only.

path.description: Vault-relative subtree. Empty means vault root.

samples.description: Filename samples per folder for overview mode.

capture_source.description:
Capture raw source material and optionally return compile guidance.

The raw source is preserved first. If `compile_guidance=true`, Exomem then
returns a proposal for a future compiled note, without silently converting
raw provenance into a conclusion.

slug.description: Optional lowercase ASCII kebab-case filename component.

source_type.description: article, session, book, paper, video, or other.

url.description: Required for article, paper, or video sources.

compile_guidance.description: Return a compilation proposal for the captured source.

suggested_title.description: Optional title hint for the compilation proposal.

compile_source.description:
Plan a compiled note from one or more raw sources.

This is read-only: it returns a note skeleton, suggested source links, and
adjacent compiled pages. The agent or user still writes the conclusion via
`remember`.

sources.description: Source paths or wikilinks to compile from.

connect_memory.description:
Connect memory through links, typed graph context, or entities.

Proposal modes are read-only. `operation="create-entity"` is an explicit
additive write that creates a typed graph node through the canonical entity
writer. `operation="accept-relation"` is a governed additive write that
authors one reviewed relation-queue candidate.

operation.description: context, suggest-links, suggest-relations, graph-context, inbound-links, create-entity, or accept-relation.

path.description: Existing page path for link, graph, or relation context.

target.description: Target path for inbound-links; defaults to path.

slug.description: Optional lowercase ASCII kebab-case entity filename component.

expected_hash.description: Target page `content_hash` drift guard for accept-relation. Required for accept-relation.

why.description: Audit reason recorded with the accept-relation edit.

expected_fingerprint.description: Reviewed candidate fingerprint for accept-relation. Required for accept-relation (not optional — a mismatch, or an omitted value, refuses the write); accept re-validates live eligibility too, so a candidate that stopped being open between the queue read and this call also refuses.

edit_memory.description:
Edit an existing memory page with an auditable reason.

Use for small corrections, section edits, batch string edits, opinion-row
fills, or one frontmatter field. Substantial rewrites should use
`replace_memory` so history stays explicit.

why.description: One-line rationale recorded in the log.

allow_curated.description: Permit frontmatter patch under curated trees.

expected_hash.description: Refuse write if content changed since read.

validate_only.description: Preview a surgical match without writing.

maintain_memory.description:
Maintain vault health with explicit write-capable modes.

Default mode is read-only audit. `mode="fix"` and `mode="backfill-ids"`
rewrite content (wikilinks, frontmatter, stable IDs) and default to
dry-run here as a safety net. `mode="reconcile"` only heals index-count
and sidecar drift from out-of-band edits — the same canonical default as
`op_reconcile` itself (idempotent, non-destructive) — so it defaults to
writing; pass `dry_run=true` to preview instead.

dry_run.description: Report without writing when true. Defaults to true for fix/backfill-ids (safety net) and false for reconcile (matches `op_reconcile`'s own default). Pass explicitly to override either way.

rebuild_embeddings.description: For fix mode, rebuild embeddings when explicitly requested.

manage_memory_file.description:
Manage files through one governed file operation.

This is the tier-2 escape hatch for structures that do not fit typed
memory commands. Destructive operations require the same explicit flags as
their canonical leaves.

operation.description: list, create, append, move, delete, trash-list, or recover.

path.description: Path for list/create/append/delete, or default recover trash path.

overwrite.description: Allow create to replace an existing file.

allow_curated.description: Permit operations in curated trees where canonical leaves allow it.

parents.description: Create parent folders in dir mode.

expected_dead_inbound.description: Links expected to die in the same workflow.

validate_only.description: Validate a Markdown create-file operation without writing.

draft_hash.description: Exact reviewed draft hash returned by validate_only.

draft_token.description: Opaque destination/date token returned by validate_only.

relation_disposition.description: Reviewed relation outcome for semantic file creation.

relation_review_hash.description: Draft hash covered by the relation review.

relation_review_reason.description: Audit reason for a reviewed-none disposition.

observe_memory.description:
Validate or mutate one semantic unit on a compiled memory page.

Compact observation is the default form. Supply an explicit governed
non-observation `kind` for rich semantic-block form and typed relations.
Use `validate` before a guarded commit when semantic review is required.

path.description: Parent page path or canonical memory reference.

kind.description: Optional governed rich kind; omitted means compact observation.

tags.description: Optional compact suffix tags or rich metadata tags.

context.description: Optional compact suffix context or rich metadata context.

relations.description: Rich typed relations as {kind, target} objects.

unit_ref.description: Current exact unit reference for update/remove or update validation.

expected_fingerprint.description: Current exact unit fingerprint; required for update/remove.

expected_hash.description: Current exact parent-page content hash; required for update/remove.

transition_token.description: Exact transition token returned by validate.

preserve_evidence.description:
Preserve text evidence as append-only proof material.

Use for receipts, letters, transcripts, warranty records, legal/dispute
material, and other factual artifacts. Binary files use `transfer_artifact`
plus the `/upload` endpoint so bytes do not pass through the model.

scope.description: Incident, case, project, or domain key.

content.description: UTF-8 text to preserve as received.

process_media.description:
Process, inspect, or retry governed media without waiting for extraction.

Supported media copied into the governed Knowledge Base or uploaded through
Exomem is processed automatically. Use this action to reconcile one artifact
immediately, inspect bounded durable status, or retry actionable blocked/failed
work after remediation. Existing valid transcripts are preserved.

path.description: Optional governed Knowledge Base media path. Omit for bounded all-media work.

query_dataset.description:
Query a CSV, TSV, or JSON dataset under the vault.

Use after `ask_memory` or `browse_memory` identifies a dataset card or raw
file. This returns exact rows or aggregates without dumping whole files.

read_media.description:
Read sampled video frames inline for visual inspection.

This is MCP-only because it returns image content blocks. Heavy media
extraction remains explicit and dependency-gated.

read_memory.description:
Read one memory page or one exact semantic unit by reference.

Use after `ask_memory` chooses a hit, or when a caller already knows the
path. With `unit_ref`, returns that exact current semantic unit, its parent
citation/lifecycle, and at most 2,400 characters of surrounding Markdown.
Missing, stale, ambiguous, and superseded references are reported through
the response `status`; no nearby unit is silently substituted. Without
`unit_ref`, this preserves the existing page-read response exactly.

path.description: Vault-relative path or Knowledge Base-relative shorthand.

frontmatter_only.description: Return only frontmatter for cheap scanning.

links.description: Include inbound and outbound wikilink summaries.

include_raw.description: Include the raw markdown file text.

unit_ref.description: Exact unit reference returned by unit-level recall. Page-only expansion flags are not accepted together with an exact unit read.

remember.description:
Remember a durable conclusion as compiled governed knowledge.

This is for distilled thinking, decisions, findings, failures, patterns,
experiments, and production logs. Raw material belongs in `capture_source`;
proof artifacts belong in `preserve_evidence`.

content.description: Full markdown body to write after frontmatter.

title.description: Unicode display title stored in frontmatter and the H1.

slug.description: Optional lowercase ASCII kebab-case filename component.

note_type.description: research-note, insight, failure, pattern, experiment, or production-log.

project.description: Required for research-note. Any slug-shaped key is accepted; unknown keys auto-register on first use (a typo guard rejects near-misses within edit distance 2 of an existing key) and create the matching Notes/Research/<Folder>/. Pass project_category to bucket a new key. Current keys (not exhaustive): personal, project-alpha, project-beta, work.

projects.description: Optional project keys for cross-project notes. Any slug-shaped key is accepted; unknown keys auto-register on first use (a typo guard rejects near-misses within edit distance 2 of an existing key) and create the matching Notes/Research/<Folder>/. Pass project_category to bucket a new key. Current keys (not exhaustive): personal, project-alpha, project-beta, work.

sources.description: Source/evidence paths this conclusion draws from.

suggestions.description: Include link suggestions in the result.

project_category.description: Category for a new project key.

validate_only.description: Validate and return an immutable creation draft without writing.

draft_hash.description: Exact reviewed draft hash returned by validate_only.

draft_token.description: Opaque destination/date token returned by validate_only.

relation_review_hash.description: Draft hash covered by the relation review.

relation_review_reason.description: Audit reason for a reviewed-none disposition.

replace_memory.description:
Supersede an existing compiled memory with a new version.

The old page remains readable and points to the new page. Use this for
meaningful changes in conclusion, not small edits.

content.description: Full markdown body for the new page.

slug.description: Optional lowercase ASCII kebab-case filename component.

reason.description: Why the old page is being superseded.

sources.description: Source/evidence paths for the new conclusion.

project_category.description: Category for a new project key.

validate_only.description: Validate the replacement draft without writing either page.

draft_hash.description: Exact reviewed draft hash returned by validate_only.

draft_token.description: Opaque destination/date token returned by validate_only.

relation_review_hash.description: Draft hash covered by the relation review.

relation_review_reason.description: Audit reason for a reviewed-none disposition.

review_item_context.description:
Inspect one stable review item with bounded recorded context.

Resolves an Inbox or corpus-activation item by `exomem://review/<id>` and
composes its target, related summaries, provenance/evidence, graph, history,
and path-specific supersession evolution. This is deterministic read-only
assembly: it runs no model, makes no epistemic judgment, and never writes.

ref.description: Stable `exomem://review/<id>` reference. An `exomem://review/adoption/<id>` ref returns the bounded Adoption Studio proposal context (proposal record, live binding check, and target-page summary) instead.

expected_fingerprint.description: Optional reviewed fingerprint; a mismatch asks the caller to refresh instead of presenting stale context.

review_memory.description:
Review memory health, provenance, drift, or source backlog.

Default mode is read-only attention review. Write-capable repairs are in
`maintain_memory`, not here.

mode.description: attention, activation, item, audit, provenance, evolution, compilation, stale, contradiction, unprocessed-sources, relation-debt, relation-queue, or adoption. `relation-queue` returns the read-only, batched relation-acceptance queue (deterministic suggestion candidates grouped by source page, with signal fingerprints and coverage counters); accept a candidate via `connect_memory(operation="accept-relation")` or reject via `triage_memory`. `adoption` returns the read-only Adoption Studio proposal queue grouped per run (structured agent proposals with signal fingerprints); approve a proposal via `adoption_studio(action="apply-proposal")` or dismiss via `triage_memory`.

path.description: Restrict provenance scan to one path.

state.description: For attention/activation, open (default), all, snoozed, or dismissed.

schema_memory.description:
Infer, validate, diff, or save governed memory schemas.

Contracts describe recurring frontmatter fields, semantic blocks, and typed
relations without changing ordinary write validation. Inference is read-only
unless `save=true`; an existing contract can only be overwritten with its
current content hash.

name.description: Lowercase contract slug; required only for `subject="contract"`.

save.description: Persist an inferred proposal. Default false.

expected_hash.description: Required current hash when overwriting a saved contract.

strict.description: In validate mode, signal a failing CLI/CI outcome on findings.

compare_to.description: In diff mode, compare to this saved contract instead of corpus reality.

proposal.description: Reviewed semantic-language, relation, or traversal-profile proposal.

transfer_artifact.description:
Prepare out-of-band binary artifact transfer.

Returns a short-lived token and URL for uploading evidence binaries or
downloading a vault file into a sandbox. The long-lived server secret never
leaves the server.

triage_memory.description:
Triage one Epistemic Inbox item explicitly.

This is the write-capable companion to read-only `review_memory`. Decisions
bind to the current signal fingerprint, so materially changed knowledge
resurfaces automatically.

ref.description: Stable `exomem://review/<id>` reference from review_memory. An `exomem://review/adoption/<id>` ref triages an Adoption Studio proposal instead, keyed the same way (`review_id:fingerprint`).

until.description: Snooze-through date as YYYY-MM-DD; required only for snooze.

why.description: Optional short rationale stored with the review decision.

expected_fingerprint.description: Optional reviewed fingerprint; a mismatch refuses the write and asks the caller to refresh.
